stem: A graph gives the estimated number of magazines y, in thousands, a publisher sent to its subscribers at the end of each year, where x represents the number of years since the end of 2005, where 0 ≤ x ≤ 12. The graph passes through the point (0, 12) and decreases as x increases. Which statement is the best interpretation of the y-intercept in this context?

answerOptions.content: The estimated total number of magazines the publisher sent during the first 12 years was 12,000.
The estimated total number of magazines the publisher sent from the end of 2005 to the end of 2017 was 120.
The estimated number of magazines the publisher sent at the end of 2005 was 12,000.
The estimated number of magazines the publisher sent at the end of 2005 was 12.

rationale: Choice C is correct. The y-intercept is the point where x = 0, which corresponds to the end of 2005. The y-coordinate of the intercept is 12, and the units are thousands of magazines. So at the end of 2005 the publisher sent approximately 12,000 magazines. Choices A and B mistake an annual count for a multi-year total. Choice D uses the wrong units.
